data.title: Transthoracic Echocardiography of Cardiac Function of Parturients in Labor
data.brief_summary: This echocardiography study will characterize heart structure and function during labor. Imaging of the heart during the stress of labor could increase detection of subclinical cardiovascular disease using advanced imaging techniques. Cardiac serum biomarkers and complication rates will also be measured and compared between patients with and without evidence of cardiovascular disease on echocardiography.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* All healthy parturients 18 years of age or older with estimated gestation greater than or equal to 37 weeks who are expected to deliver during their hospitalization.
* During the echocardiographic exam, the patient must be in active labor with cervical dilation between 6-10 cm
endpoints: The proportion of patients with an abnormal strain pattern as compared to parturients not in labor
The proportion of patients with diastolic dysfunction as compared to parturients not in labor
